stats_fallback.christened: 2022-12-07 (Manhattan Cruise Terminal, New York; godmother Sophia Loren appearing via special broadcast; Alexa Aponte-Vago as in-person ribbon cutter)
stats_fallback.beam: 135 ft (41 m waterline; 50 m max extreme breadth)
stats_fallback.yacht_club_status: Installed from 2022 delivery (Seaside-class ships had Yacht Club from build). ~32,000 sq ft forward enclave with Concierge Area and Top Sail Lounge (Deck 16), MSC Yacht Club Restaurant (Deck 18), private sundeck (Deck 20) with The One Pool, The One Pool Grill & Bar, and MSC Yacht Club Grill & Bar. 24-hour butler service.

related.class_notes: Seaside EVO Class. MSC Seascape is the second and final Seaside EVO-class ship; her only sister is MSC Seashore (2021). The Seaside EVO class is an enlarged evolution of the original Seaside Class (MSC Seaside 2017 + MSC Seaview 2018 — parent class, not EVO sisters). The EVO pair are 170,412 GT vs ~153,516 GT for the parent class, have 2,270 cabins, and carry four main dining rooms (Green Wave, Cala Jondal, Aegean, Skyline) instead of the usual two MSC MDRs.

deployment.nov_2025_repositioning: 7 November 2025: repositioned from Miami to Galveston, Texas — MSC Cruises' first-ever Texas homeport. Inaugurated the Port of Galveston's new $156M Cruise Terminal 16. First Galveston revenue sailing: Sunday 9 November 2025.
deployment.2026_through_2027: Year-round 7-night Western Caribbean round-trips from Galveston, Texas (Cruise Terminal 16). Weekly Sunday turnarounds. Ports of call: Costa Maya (Mexico), Cozumel (Mexico), Roatán (Honduras). Scheduled through at least October 2027. Texas-themed elements: Lone Star resident country band, line-dance classes, Big Texas Sailaway party, Southern/Texan/Gulf Coast dining additions.
deployment.current_ais: 11 April 2026: returning from Cozumel to Galveston for Sunday 12 April turnaround, consistent with weekly rotation. Speed 22.0 kn, draught 8.6 m.

history.christened: 7 December 2022, Manhattan Cruise Terminal, New York City (Sophia Loren via special broadcast; Alexa Aponte-Vago as in-person ribbon cutter)
history.class_position: Second and final Seaside EVO-class ship
history.incidents: 31 May 2025 — Ocean Cay, Bahamas mooring incident: sudden squall broke mooring lines; ship drifted from pier, gangway dislodged into water; passengers returning aboard were restricted from boarding; one reported injury. Ship returned to Miami on schedule 1 June. No other notable incidents.

discrepancies_flagged.imo_contamination: IMO 9839777 sometimes found in older or third-party data for Seascape is INCORRECT — that IMO belongs to the Israeli tug LUBRANI (498 GT). Correct MSC Seascape IMO is 9843807. This is the fifth IMO cross-contamination caught across the MSC fleet repair.
discrepancies_flagged.deployment_outdated: Many sources still list MSC Seascape as Miami-based year-round. As of 7 November 2025 she is Galveston-based year-round. Always verify against current MSC and Port of Galveston releases.
discrepancies_flagged.beam: 41 m waterline (CruiseMapper, Wikipedia) vs 50 m extreme (VesselFinder, VesselTracker). Both correct: 41 m is the waterline beam, 50 m is the max beam including upper-deck overhangs. Use 41 m as primary.
discrepancies_flagged.chefs_court: Not a separate branded restaurant but the name of the specialty-restaurants cluster on Deck 8 (grouping Butcher's Cut, Kaito Sushi Bar, Kaito Teppanyaki, Hola! Tacos & Cantina, Ocean Cay Restaurant around a shared Cocktail Bar). Treat as the specialty quarter.
discrepancies_flagged.class_confusion: Some sources call Seascape a 'Seaside-class' ship; technically she is 'Seaside EVO Class' (a sub-class of Seaside). Use 'Seaside EVO Class' as the precise and MSC-official name.
discrepancies_flagged.sisters: Many sources incorrectly list MSC Seaside / Seaview as sisters. Wrong — Seascape's only sister is MSC Seashore. Seaside and Seaview are parent-class, not EVO.
